channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: US-Canada-Mexico joint World Cup goes from unity to acrimony thanks to tariffs and '51st state' talk
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Seven years ago, when a joint bid by the United States, Canada and Mexico was awarded the 2026 World Cup, rifts created by tariffs — yes, back then, too! — and a proposed border wall were glossed over because of the neighbors’ longstanding political and economic alliances.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: In recruiting battle, lawmakers weigh whether to give college athletes a tax break on NIL earnings
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Illinois state Rep. Travis Weaver remembers how surreal it felt having Heisman Trophy winner Mark Ingram sit a few rows behind him in a political science class. He will never forget the pure joy in Alabama every time the Crimson Tide rolled to the national championship, either.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Women's March Madness Final Four: What to know
item.description: Paige Buckers, Madison Booker and Dawn Staley highlight the biggest names left in the NCAA women's Final Four. Here is a breakdown of the last teams standing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Athletics bat boy Stewart Thalblum takes down drone in left field
item.description: WEST SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — When a drone suddenly appeared near the left-field wall at Sutter Health Park on Monday night, veteran Athletics bat boy Stewart Thalblum decided to help thwart it.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Carson Kelly becomes 1st Cubs player to hit for the cycle in 32 years
item.description: WEST SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — Carson Kelly had come close to hitting for the cycle, yet that elusive triple kept holding him back. He had just two triples over a 10-year big league career before Monday night. That's when Kelly became the first Chicago Cubs player in 32 years to hit for the cycle.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A's routed by Cubs 18-3 in Sacramento home opener
item.description: WEST SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) -- Carson Kelly hit for the cycle and drove in five runs, Michael Busch launched a two-run homer on the way to a career-high four RBIs, and the Chicago Cubs pounded the Athletics 18-3 on Monday night to spoil their first game in their new Northern California home.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Favorite UConn joins 3 top seeds in South Carolina, UCLA and Texas in women’s Final Four
item.description: No room for Cinderellas in Tampa this week with No. 1 seeds South Carolina, UCLA and Texas in the national semifinals.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Madison Booker helps Texas reach its first women's Final Four since 2003 with 58-47 win over TCU
item.description: BIRMINGHAM, Ala. (AP) — Rori Harmon put her hands on her knees and bowed her head as the clock wound down. The Texas senior guard doesn't like crying, but she gave herself a moment to reflect on the past 10 months. This time last year, Harmon was sidelined by a knee injury.
item.dc:creator: ALANIS THAMES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Celtics beat Grizzlies 117-103 and complete the first 6-0 trip in franchise history
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Jayson Tatum had 25 points and 14 rebounds, Al Horford scored 26 points and the Boston Celtics swept a six-game road trip for the first time in franchise history with a 117-103 win over the Memphis Grizzlies on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: CLAY BAILEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 players, 2 coaches ejected after Pistons-Timberwolves skirmish in 2nd quarter
item.description: Five players and two coaches were ejected after a fight broke out in the second quarter in the Minnesota Timberwolves' victory over the Detroit Pistons on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK DONNELLY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Champions Retreat Golf Club ready to host Augusta National Women's Amateur following Hurricane Helene
item.description: The first practice round of the Augusta National Women's Amateur and other Masters related activities return to Georgia after Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Barclay Bishop, Fox 5

item.title: Kyren Paris hits solo home run after long weather delay to lift Angels over White Sox 3-2
item.description: Kyren Paris homered on the first pitch he saw after a lengthy rain delay and the Los Angeles Angels rallied to beat the Chicago White Sox 3-2 on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Utah snaps 3-game skid with 5-2 win at Chicago
item.description: CHICAGO (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Hockey Club continues to fight for its slim playoff hopes. Alex Kerfoot had a goal and two assists for the Utah Hockey Club in a 5-2 victory against the Chicago Blackhawks at United Center on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Duke and Florida move on to Final Four
item.description: Florida and Duke won their regions on Saturday, with fellow top seeds Houston and Auburn set to play on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ilia Malinin cruises to 2nd straight title to cap an American gold rush at figure skating worlds
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Ilia Malinin walked toward the ice for his free skate at the world championships with the swagger of a conquering hero. Then he backed up the bravado.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lightning strike early and often in 8-0 win over Utah HC
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Hockey Club will need close to a miracle to make the NHL playoffs now. After suffering its worst blowout loss of the season, coupled with a St.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Baseball: Point Loma 13, Montgomery 6
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- Out on the point a high scoring baseball game between Point Loma and Montgomery. The Pointers hold on to win 13-6. Point Loma jumped out to an 8-2 lead, but Montgomery quickly scored four runs to cut it to 8-6.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: New San Diego Padres mural in North Park nears completion
item.description: A new San Diego Padres-themed mural in North Park replacing a previous work of public art that had been defaced earlier this year by fans of the rival Los Angeles Dodgers is nearing completion, just in time for opening weekend.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: SF's Chase Center hosting NCAA Men's Basketball games Thursday, Saturday
item.description: (BCN) -- San Francisco is hosting two games at Chase Center on Thursday in the NCAA Men's Basketball Championship tournament. At 4:39 p.m., University of Florida, which is the No. 1 seed in the West region of the tournament's bracket, plays No. 4 University of Maryland.
item.dc:creator: Bay City News, KRON4

item.title: Runnin' Utes name Chris Wilcox general manager
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Utah head men's basketball coach Alex Jensen continues to fill his staff, announcing the addition of Sacramento Kings Assistant GM Wes Wilcox as the newest general manager for the Runnin' Utes program.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Major League Cricket coming to Oakland Coliseum
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (KRON) -- In the East Bay, the City of Oakland has announced that Major League Cricket will play matches at the Coliseum this summer. Cricket is the world’s second most popular sport, after soccer.
item.dc:creator: Charles Clifford, KRON4

item.title: San Diego State Aztec guard declares for NBA Draft
item.description: San Diego State Aztecs men's basketball player Miles Byrd is looking to make his next move to the professional level.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: Tennessee pitcher Karlyn Pickens throws fastest pitch in college softball history
item.description: Tennessee softball standout Karlyn Pickens wrote her name into the history books on Monday with a record-breaking pitch.
item.dc:creator: Gregory Raucoules, WGN 9
